results.demotion_reason: Deep-dive Item 2 (successor verification) reveals: while live team page at vannoyassoc.com lists only Ray Vannoy + claims '16 former employees became RPLS', cross-reference of search engine results identifies KALVIN WALKER as 'President' of Vannoy surveying operations and confirms 3 active offices (Waco 7111 Bosque Blvd 76710 + Tyler 104 Jordan Plaza Blvd Ste 401 + Terrell 306 N Adelaide 75160). Ray Vannoy is no longer the operating president — Kalvin Walker is. This is a non-family co-principal succession candidate already in place. Per skill rule (anyone with operations title + multi-year tenure is a successor candidate), demote A→B. Ray Vannoy is past 50yr tenure (RPLS 1978); Walker is the heir-apparent / current operating president.

items_checked.1_owner_age: Ray Vannoy ~78 (RPLS 1978 + ~30 age at license + ~18 years = 78). License-tenure proxy only; OV65 not verified.
items_checked.2_successor_verification: FAILED — Kalvin Walker is President of surveying operations per cross-referenced search results; not just an associate but the operating principal. Internal-succession in place.
items_checked.3_comptroller_status: Unverified via direct CAD/Comptroller lookup; firm shows active operations (3 offices, current website, current LinkedIn).
items_checked.7_value_add_thesis: Multi-office + oil/gas + pipeline + litigation = specialty book is valuable, but the Walker succession in place means an outside buyer competes with internal-buy-out.
items_checked.8_metro_pull: Multi-office TX = broad exposure; not metro-pull-recomputed.

data_sources_added.source: Cross-referenced web search confirming Kalvin Walker as President + 3 offices

items_checked.2_successor_verification: PASSED — floresengineers.com/about-us/ live-fetch (2026-05-16) confirms 'Mr. Flores acquired sole ownership' after partner departure; no son / daughter / Jr / successor listed anywhere. No DBA in firm name suggesting succession. Multi-discipline (engineering + surveying) with no public second principal.

items_checked.4_disciplinary: No disciplinary action surfaced in search.
items_checked.6_building_ownership: Bexar County operations address 12915 Jones Maltsberger Suite 401 — leased space (suite #), not owned. No real-estate-heavy thesis here.
items_checked.8_metro_pull: San Antonio Bexar County metro + 150mi radius service area + up-to-16,000-acre projects = real recurring B2B. Hispanic-owned background gives SBA HUB/Minority program eligibility = SBA-acquisition tailwind.

results.value_add_thesis_sharpened: Thomas Flores, 81, sole principal P.E.+RPLS of Flores & Co Consulting Engineers (San Antonio, est 1982, 43yr) — no successor on team page, no son/daughter/Jr listed, classic exit-window solo principal with multi-discipline civil engineering + land surveying franchise. Specific value-add (1) digitization: no drone, no RTK, no laser scanning, no 3D modeling mentioned on site = $50-150K capex unlocks 25-40% labor savings on topo + as-built; (2) recurring construction stake-out program with SA-area homebuilders (KB Home, DR Horton, Lennar Texas) — not yet visible in client logos but Bexar County development volume supports it; (3) HUB/MBE/DBE certification likely (Flores founder, Hispanic heritage) = recurring municipal + TxDOT contract eligibility worth 1.5-2× the EBITDA multiple of a non-certified shop; (4) Army Corps of Engineers founder background + 16,000-acre project capability = differentiated capacity for institutional + federal work; (5) succession risk solved by acquire-self thesis. Plausible 1.5-2× EBITDA path 18-24 months; off-market multiple 3-5× EBITDA in current LOW-PE-attention surveying environment.

TBPELS RPLS roster confirms solo-RPLS at firm 10034800. Limited business hours (Mon-Thu 8-5, Fri 8-4) = winding-down posture.

items_checked.7_value_add_thesis: (See sharpened thesis.) CAVEAT: At age 90 with no successor, risk of operator-incapacitation event before deal closes is material — this is more of a 'fast-moving acquire-self' thesis than 'classic 36-month exit-window' thesis.
items_checked.8_metro_pull: Bryan/College Station Brazos County secondary metro — Texas A&M University tied, growing recurring municipal + university construction work. Service area = Brazos + Burleson + Madison + Grimes + Robertson counties (5-county footprint).

results.value_add_thesis_sharpened: Dante Carlomagno, 90, Argentina-born immigrant solo principal of Carlomagno Surveying Inc (Bryan/College Station, est 1973, 53yr) with no successor on team page, no son/daughter/Jr listed, no associate; firm classified as classic exit-window solo principal at extreme age = high-urgency acquire-self target. Specific value-add (1) extreme operator-age urgency: 90-year-old principal materially increases probability of imminent transition; an outside buyer with capital and transition plan has structural advantage over the typical 'wait for broker' approach; (2) Bryan/College Station = Texas A&M University-tied secondary metro with steady recurring municipal + university construction + agricultural-land boundary work in 5-county service area; (3) NO modern tech-stack visible on site (no drone, no RTK GPS, no laser scanning, no online portal) = $30-80K capex unlocks 25-40% labor savings and 1.5-2× growth runway; (4) 53yr firm tenure = built-in customer loyalty + multi-generation referral base in a small market; (5) the firm name + brand recognition is the durable asset (Brazos County 5-county footprint has limited surveying capacity). Plausible 1.5-2.5× EBITDA path 18-24 months; off-market multiple 2.5-4× EBITDA given operator-age urgency and seller-financed assumption.

data_sources_added.source: OpenCorporates TX SOS Carlomagno Surveying Inc

results.value_add_thesis_sharpened: Richard V. Hall, ~78 (RPLS since 1978, license-tenure proxy), solo principal of Prime Texas Surveys (Houston HQ + Mission TX office, est ~1978, 48yr) — no individual successor or co-RPLS on TBPELS roster or live team page; multi-office expansion suggests appetite for growth but capacity-bound by single RPLS. Specific value-add (1) RPLS-staffing expansion: bring in 2-3 RPLS to unlock 2-3× revenue capacity across the Houston + Mission + Rockport + RGV footprint already established; (2) Greater Houston FEMA elevation cert program + Harris-Galveston Subsidence District annual monitoring contracts = $200-500K/yr recurring annuity book worth 1.5-2× the EBITDA multiple of a one-off-only shop; (3) site already modernized (online ordering, 2026 copyright, 'Request a Free Quote' funnel) — operational digital infrastructure is the rare strength here, unlike most TX surveying firms; the leverage is in capacity, not digital transformation; (4) Mission TX + RGV office = Hispanic-market expansion path + recurring municipal boundary work; (5) 48yr brand recognition in Greater Houston is a durable asset. Plausible 1.5-2× EBITDA path 12-18 months focused on capacity expansion (RPLS hiring) + recurring-program acquisition; off-market multiple 3-5× EBITDA.
